-- 1. Add a new column with the desired type (TEXT) and a default value
ALTER TABLE team_attributes ADD COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass_new TEXT;

-- 2. Create a CHECK constraint on the new column to enforce the allowed values
-- This replaces the strictness of an ENUM. You can include 'Very Fast' here immediately.
ALTER TABLE team_attributes ADD CONSTRAINT check_buildupplayspeedclass_new 
CHECK (buildupplayspeedclass_new IN ('Slow', 'Balanced', 'Fast', 'Very Fast'));

-- 3. Copy data from the old column to the new column in batches
-- This avoids holding a lock on the table for the duration of the update.
-- Repeat this statement adjusting the LIMIT and OFFSET until all rows are updated.
-- Example batch:
UPDATE team_attributes
SET buildupplayspeedclass_new = buildupplayspeedclass
WHERE id IS NOT NULL
  AND buildupplayspeedclass_new IS NULL
LIMIT 10000;

-- 4. Once all data is migrated, swap the columns in a single, short transaction
BEGIN;
-- Rename the old column out of the way
ALTER TABLE team_attributes RENAME COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass TO buildupplayspeedclass_old;
-- Rename the new column to the original name
ALTER TABLE team_attributes RENAME COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass_new TO buildupplayspeedclass;
COMMIT;

-- 5. (Optional) Drop the old column and constraint
-- This can be done later during a maintenance window.
ALTER TABLE team_attributes DROP COLUMN buildupplayspeedclass_old

WITH RECURSIVE UserGroups AS (
    -- Anchor: Select each user as the start of a group
    SELECT 
        id AS group_id, 
        id AS user_id, 
        username, 
        ref1, 
        ref2
    FROM users
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive Step: Find users connected via ref1 or ref2
    SELECT 
        g.group_id, 
        u.id, 
        u.username, 
        u.ref1, 
        u.ref2
    FROM UserGroups g
    JOIN users u ON 
        g.ref1 = u.ref1 OR 
        g.ref1 = u.ref2 OR 
        g.ref2 = u.ref1 OR 
        g.ref2 = u.ref2
),
GroupedUsers AS (
    -- Aggregate users into their connected components
    SELECT 
        group_id,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT user_id) AS ids,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT username) AS usernames,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ref1) AS refs1,
        ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ref2) AS refs2
    FROM UserGroups
    GROUP BY group_id
)
-- Join groups with assets and calculate counts
SELECT 
    gu.ids, 
    gu.usernames, 
    gu.refs1, 
    gu.refs2, 
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS asset_count
FROM GroupedUsers gu
LEFT JOIN assets a ON a.owner = ANY(gu.refs1) OR a.owner = ANY(gu.refs2)
GROUP BY gu.group_id, gu.ids, gu.usernames, gu.refs1, gu.refs2
ORDER BY MIN(a.id)

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
    -- Anchor member: Select cards that have no previous card (start of the chain)
    SELECT 
        c1.id, 
        c1.nextcardid, 
        c1.id::TEXT AS path
    FROM collection c1
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM collection c2 
        WHERE c2.nextcardid = c1.id
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive member: Traverse to the next card in the chain
    SELECT 
        c.id, 
        c.nextcardid, 
        p.path || ' --> ' || c.id::TEXT
    FROM collection c
    JOIN path_cte p ON c.id = p.nextcardid
)
-- Select only the paths that have reached the end of the chain (nextcardid is NULL)
SELECT path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextcardid IS NULL
ORDER BY id

WITH monthly_purchases AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id, 
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS month_date
    FROM purchase
    GROUP BY user_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_DATE(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD'))
),
streak_groups AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id, 
        month_date,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) - 
        EXTRACT(MONTH FROM AGE(month_date, '1900-01-01')) AS grp
    FROM monthly_purchases
),
user_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id, 
        COUNT(*) AS streak_length
    FROM streak_groups
    GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
longest_streaks AS (
    SELECT 
        user_id, 
        MAX(streak_length) AS longest_streak
    FROM user_streaks
    GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT 
    longest_streak, 
    COUNT(*) AS num_users
FROM longest_streaks
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY longest_streak

WITH extracted_cardid AS (
    SELECT 
        -- Extract the pure card ID by removing the substring between the first and second hyphens
        -- Logic: Take the part before the first hyphen, concatenate with the part after the second hyphen
        SUBSTRING(cardid FROM 1 FOR POSITION('-' IN cardid) - 1) || 
        SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN SUBSTRING(cardid FROM POSITION('-' IN cardid) + 1)) + POSITION('-' IN cardid) + 1) AS pure_cardid,
        type
    FROM 
        card_info
),
min_type_per_card AS (
    SELECT 
        pure_cardid,
        -- Retrieve the minimum value of type for each unique pure_cardid
        MIN(type) AS min_type
    FROM 
        extracted_cardid
    GROUP BY 
        pure_cardid
)
SELECT 
    pure_cardid,
    min_type
FROM 
    min_type_per_card

  CAST(SPLIT_PART(SUBSTRING(version, '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+'), '.', 3) AS INTEGER) DESC,
  -- Extract Build version (e.g., 1 from 3.0.5-1-test-dev)

  -- Sort by the remaining suffix alphabetically (e.g., 'test-dev')

WITH signed_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_date,
        club_name,
        amount,
        SIGN(amount) as amount_sign,
        -- Calculate the row number for the partition
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) as rn
    FROM club_transactions
),
grouped_transactions AS (
    SELECT 
        transaction_date,
        club_name,
        amount,
        amount_sign,
        -- Subtract the row number from the sum of the row number and a flag (0 or 1) 
        -- to create a unique group ID that changes only when the sign changes
        SUM(CASE WHEN amount_sign = LAG(amount_sign) OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) 
                 THEN 0 
                 ELSE 1 
            END) OVER (PARTITION BY club_name ORDER BY transaction_date) as grp
    FROM signed_transactions
)
SELECT 
    transaction_date,
    club_name,
    SUM(amount) OVER (
        PARTITION BY club_name, amount_sign, grp 
        ORDER BY transaction_date
        ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
    ) as total_amount
FROM grouped_transactions

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl text, id text, row_num text) 
RETURNS void 
LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $func$
DECLARE
    col_list text[];
    col text;
    sql_stmt text;
BEGIN
    -- Get list of nullable columns for the specified table
    SELECT array_agg(column_name) 
    INTO col_list
    FROM information_schema.columns 
    WHERE table_name = tbl 
      AND is_nullable = 'YES';

    -- Loop through each nullable column
    FOREACH col IN ARRAY col_list LOOP
        -- Construct and execute dynamic SQL to update the column using forward fill
        sql_stmt := format(
            'UPDATE %I t1 
             SET %I = (
                 SELECT last_value(%I) OVER (
                     ORDER BY %I 
                     ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
                 )
                 FROM %I t2 
                 WHERE t2.%I = t1.%I 
                   AND t2.%I <= t1.%I
                 ORDER BY t2.%I DESC
                 LIMIT 1
             )
             WHERE t1.%I IS NULL;',
            tbl, col, col, row_num, tbl, id, id, row_num, row_num, row_num, col
        );
        
        EXECUTE sql_stmt;
    END LOOP;
END;
$func$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.generate_series_monthly(a date, b date)
RETURNS SETOF date
LANGUAGE SQL
IMMUTABLE PARALLEL SAFE
ROWS 10
AS $function$
  SELECT generate_series(date_trunc('month', a), date_trunc('month', b), '1 month'::interval)

WITH MatchGroups AS (
    SELECT 
        m.id,
        m.date,
        m.home_team_goal,
        m.away_team_goal,
        -- Create a running total of draws to identify groups of matches between draws
        SUM(CASE WHEN m.home_team_goal = m.away_team_goal THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) 
            OVER (ORDER BY m.id, m.date) AS group_id
    FROM match AS m
),
GroupSums AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        date,
        home_team_goal,
        away_team_goal,
        -- Calculate the total goals for each group
        SUM(home_team_goal + away_team_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY group_id) AS total_goals
    FROM MatchGroups
)
-- Select only the draw matches with their accumulated goals
SELECT 
    id,
    date,
    home_team_goal,
    away_team_goal,
    total_goals AS amount
FROM GroupSums
WHERE home_team_goal = away_team_goal
ORDER BY id, date

WITH RankedTransactions AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid,
        transactionid,
        gasstationid,
        amount,
        date,
        time,
        -- Identify the last transaction for each customer at each gas station
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid 
            ORDER BY date DESC, time DESC
        ) AS station_rank,
        -- Identify the first transaction (chronologically) where amount < 10 for each customer
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY customerid 
            ORDER BY 
                CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 ELSE 2 END, -- Prioritize issues
                date ASC, 
                time ASC
        ) AS issue_rank
    FROM transactions_1k
)
SELECT 
    customerid,
    transactionid,
    gasstationid,
    amount
FROM RankedTransactions
WHERE 
    -- Rule 1: The last transaction at each gas station
    station_rank = 1
    -- Rule 2: If there is an issue (amount < 10), keep only the first gas station where it occurred
    AND (
        (issue_rank = 1 AND amount < 10) 
        OR 
        -- Rule 3: If no issues, keep all last transactions (which includes the last station with amount >= 10)
        NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1 
            FROM RankedTransactions rt2 
            WHERE rt2.customerid = RankedTransactions.customerid 
            AND rt2.amount < 10
        )
    )

WITH RECURSIVE
-- 1. Create a unified list of edges (item, subgroup)
edges AS (
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup1 AS subgroup FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT id AS item, subgroup2 AS subgroup FROM superhero_group WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
-- 2. Find the minimum item ID for each subgroup to serve as a root
roots AS (
    SELECT subgroup, MIN(item) AS root_item FROM edges GROUP BY subgroup
),
-- 3. Recursive traversal to find all connected items
traversal AS (
    -- Base case: items are roots of their own group initially
    SELECT item, item AS group_id FROM edges
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive step: if an item shares a subgroup with an item already visited, 
    -- adopt the group_id of the visited item.
    SELECT e.item, t.group_id
    FROM edges e
    JOIN roots r ON e.subgroup = r.subgroup
    JOIN traversal t ON r.root_item = t.item
    WHERE e.item != t.item -- Prevent infinite loops on self
),
-- 4. Assign a final unique ID to each connected component
final_groups AS (
    SELECT item, MIN(group_id) as min_group_id FROM traversal GROUP BY item
)
-- 5. Join back to the original data to present the result
SELECT 
    s.id AS item, 
    s.subgroup1, 
    s.subgroup2, 
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY fg.min_group_id) AS new_group_id
FROM superhero_group s
LEFT JOIN final_groups fg ON s.id = fg.item

WITH report_aggregated AS (
    -- 1. Aggregate by the full granularity (id, date, all 5 utm params)
    SELECT 
        id, 
        date_of_visit, 
        utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term,
        SUM(sessions) AS sessions, 
        SUM(pageviews) AS pageviews, 
        SUM(bounces) AS bounces
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- 2. Aggregate by id, date, campaign, source, medium (content and term are NULL)
    SELECT 
        id, 
        date_of_visit, 
        utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium, 
        NULL AS utm_content, 
        NULL AS utm_term,
        SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces)
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium
    
    UNION ALL

    -- 3. Aggregate by id, date, campaign, source (medium, content, term are NULL)
    SELECT 
        id, 
        date_of_visit, 
        utm_campaign, utm_source, 
        NULL AS utm_medium, 
        NULL AS utm_content, 
        NULL AS utm_term,
        SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces)
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign, utm_source

    UNION ALL

    -- 4. Aggregate by id, date, campaign (source, medium, content, term are NULL)
    SELECT 
        id, 
        date_of_visit, 
        utm_campaign, 
        NULL AS utm_source, 
        NULL AS utm_medium, 
        NULL AS utm_content, 
        NULL AS utm_term,
        SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces)
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit, utm_campaign
    
    UNION ALL

    -- 5. Aggregate by id, date only (all utm params are NULL)
    SELECT 
        id, 
        date_of_visit, 
        NULL AS utm_campaign, 
        NULL AS utm_source, 
        NULL AS utm_medium, 
        NULL AS utm_content, 
        NULL AS utm_term,
        SUM(sessions), SUM(pageviews), SUM(bounces)
    FROM report
    GROUP BY id, date_of_visit
)
SELECT 
    u.row_id AS id,
    u.utm_campaign,
    u.utm_source,
    u.utm_medium,
    u.utm_content,
    u.utm_term,
    r.date_of_visit,
    r.sessions,
    r.pageviews,
    r.bounces
FROM utm u
JOIN report_aggregated r 
    ON u.row_id = r.id 
    AND (u.utm_campaign IS NULL OR r.utm_campaign IS NULL OR u.utm_campaign = r.utm_campaign)
    AND (u.utm_source IS NULL OR r.utm_source IS NULL OR u.utm_source = r.utm_source)
    AND (u.utm_medium IS NULL OR r.utm_medium IS NULL OR u.utm_medium = r.utm_medium)
    AND (u.utm_content IS NULL OR r.utm_content IS NULL OR u.utm_content = r.utm_content)
    AND (u.utm_term IS NULL OR r.utm_term IS NULL OR u.utm_term = r.utm_term)

-- First, we create the state transition function for the aggregate.
-- This function carries forward the last non-null value.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION forward_fill_transition(state anyelement, next_value anyelement)
RETURNS anyelement AS $$
BEGIN
    -- If the next value is not null, update the state to the next value.
    -- Otherwise, keep the current state (which holds the last non-null value).
    RETURN COALESCE(next_value, state);
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Next, we create the aggregate function using the transition function.
-- We define the initial state (STYPE) as NULL.
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION forward_fill(anyelement)
RETURNS anyelement AS $$
    SELECT forward_fill_transition($1, $2);
$$ LANGUAGE SQL;

CREATE AGGREGATE forward_fill(anyelement) (
    SFUNC = forward_fill_transition,
    STYPE = anyelement,
    INITCOND = NULL
);

-- Finally, we execute the query using the aggregate function as a window function.
-- We partition by publisher_id and order by id to ensure the fill happens correctly within each group.
SELECT 
    id, 
    publisher_id, 
    height_cm, 
    weight_kg, 
    forward_fill(height_cm) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher_id ORDER BY id) AS height_cm_filled, 
    forward_fill(weight_kg) OVER (PARTITION BY publisher_id ORDER BY id) AS weight_kg_filled 
FROM superhero

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_superhero_ids_json()
RETURNS SETOF json AS $$
DECLARE
    _column_list text;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Retrieve the list of column names ending with '_id' from the metadata
    SELECT string_agg('"' || column_name || '"', ', ')
    INTO _column_list
    FROM information_schema.columns
    WHERE table_name = 'superhero' 
      AND column_name LIKE '%_id';

    -- 2. Construct and execute a dynamic query that builds a JSON object
    -- from the identified columns for each row.
    RETURN QUERY EXECUTE format('SELECT json_build_object(%s) FROM superhero', _column_list);
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

WITH RECURSIVE craft_cost AS (
    -- Base case: items that can be crafted using only resources
    SELECT 
        s.item_id,
        s.item_price AS retail_price,
        COALESCE(s.resource_price * s.quantity, 0) AS craft_cost
    FROM 
        store s
    WHERE 
        s.uses_item_id IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive case: items that require other items
    SELECT 
        s.item_id,
        s.item_price AS retail_price,
        COALESCE(s.resource_price * s.quantity, 0) + 
        COALESCE(c.craft_cost * s.quantity, 0) AS craft_cost
    FROM 
        store s
    JOIN 
        craft_cost c ON s.uses_item_id = c.item_id
)
SELECT 
    item_id,
    retail_price,
    SUM(craft_cost) AS full_craft_price
FROM 
    craft_cost
GROUP BY 
    item_id, retail_price
ORDER BY 
    item_id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reindex_superhero_ids(publisher_id_param BIGINT) RETURNS VOID LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Create a temporary sequence for reindexing
    CREATE TEMPORARY SEQUENCE IF NOT EXISTS id_seq_temp MINVALUE 1 START WITH 1 INCREMENT BY 1;
    
    -- Reset the sequence to start from 1
    PERFORM nextval('id_seq_temp'); -- This ensures the sequence is ready
    
    -- Reindex hair_colour_ids for the given publisher
    UPDATE superhero
    SET hair_colour_id = nextval('id_seq_temp')
    WHERE publisher_id = publisher_id_param;
    
    -- Clean up the temporary sequence
    DROP SEQUENCE IF EXISTS id_seq_temp;
END;
$$

WITH RECURSIVE HierarchyCTE AS (
    -- Anchor member: Select all staff members
    SELECT 
        id AS employee_id,
        bossid,
        salary AS employee_salary,
        0 AS level
    FROM staff
    UNION ALL
    -- Recursive member: Join with the previous level to find managers
    SELECT 
        h.employee_id,
        s.bossid,
        h.employee_salary,
        h.level + 1
    FROM staff s
    INNER JOIN HierarchyCTE h ON s.id = h.bossid
),
RankedManagers AS (
    -- Filter for managers earning at least twice the employee's salary
    -- and rank them by their distance in the hierarchy (level)
    SELECT 
        employee_id,
        employee_salary,
        level,
        bossid,
        salary AS boss_salary,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY employee_id ORDER BY level ASC) as rn
    FROM HierarchyCTE h
    JOIN staff s ON h.bossid = s.id
    WHERE s.salary >= 2 * h.employee_salary
)
-- Select the first (closest) qualifying manager for each employee
SELECT 
    employee_id AS employeeId, 
    employee_salary AS employeeSalary, 
    level AS hierarchical_level, 
    bossid AS bossId, 
    boss_salary AS bossSalary
FROM RankedManagers
WHERE rn = 1

WITH RECURSIVE transfer_cte AS (
    -- Anchor member: Start with the first row based on ID
    SELECT 
        id, 
        amount, 
        amount AS cum_sum, 
        1 AS bucket
    FROM transfer
    WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM transfer)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive member: Process subsequent rows
    SELECT 
        t.id, 
        t.amount, 
        -- Reset cum_sum to current amount if threshold exceeded, else add to previous cum_sum
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN t.amount 
            ELSE c.cum_sum + t.amount 
        END AS cum_sum,
        -- Increment bucket if threshold exceeded, else keep current bucket
        CASE 
            WHEN c.cum_sum + t.amount > 20 THEN c.bucket + 1 
            ELSE c.bucket 
        END AS bucket
    FROM transfer t
    JOIN transfer_cte c ON t.id = c.id + 1
)
SELECT 
    id, 
    amount, 
    cum_sum, 
    bucket 
FROM transfer_cte
ORDER BY id

-- The issue is that the 'dependency' table has a composite foreign key referencing (id, stage) in the 'match' table.
-- Updating the 'stage' in the 'match' table violates referential integrity because the 'dependency' table still holds the old 'stage' value.
-- To fix this, we must update the 'stage' in both the 'match' table and the referencing 'dependency' table within the same transaction.
-- Since the requirement is to avoid manually starting a transaction, we can use a Common Table Expression (CTE) to perform the updates atomically.

WITH updated_match AS (
    -- Update the stage in the match table
    UPDATE match
    SET stage = stage + 1
    WHERE id = 'match1'
    RETURNING id, stage
)
-- Update the dependencies that reference the updated match as match1
UPDATE dependency
SET stage1 = updated_match.stage
FROM updated_match
WHERE dependency.match1 = updated_match.id;

WITH updated_match AS (
    -- Update the stage in the match table
    UPDATE match
    SET stage = stage + 1
    WHERE id = 'match1'
    RETURNING id, stage
)
-- Update the dependencies that reference the updated match as match2
UPDATE dependency
SET stage2 = updated_match.stage
FROM updated_match
WHERE dependency.match2 = updated_match.id

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to() RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE team_attributes
    SET eff_to = subquery.next_date
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            id,
            COALESCE(
                LEAD(TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE, 1) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY team_api_id 
                    ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(date, 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS')::DATE ASC
                ) - INTERVAL '1 day',
                TO_DATE('5999-12-31', 'YYYY-MM-DD')
            ) AS next_date
        FROM team_attributes
    ) AS subquery
    WHERE team_attributes.id = subquery.id;
    
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to()

WITH RECURSIVE tree_coloring AS (
    -- Anchor member: Select leaf nodes (nodes with no children)
    -- We assume leaf nodes already have their color (green or red)
    SELECT 
        s2.id, 
        s2.parent_id, 
        s2.color
    FROM superhero2 s2
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 
        FROM superhero2 s_child 
        WHERE s_child.parent_id = s2.id
    )
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive member: Select parents of the nodes identified in the previous step
    -- and calculate their color based on their children's colors
    SELECT
        parent.id,
        parent.parent_id,
        CASE 
            -- If one child is NULL (only one child found in the current recursion batch), 
            -- take the color of the existing child.
            WHEN child1.color IS NULL THEN child2.color
            WHEN child2.color IS NULL THEN child1.color
            -- If both children have the same color, take that color.
            WHEN child1.color = child2.color THEN child1.color
            -- If children have different colors, result is gray.
            ELSE 'gray'
        END AS color
    FROM superhero2 parent
    -- Join with the recursive CTE to find children of the current parent
    -- We join on parent.id = tc.parent_id because tc contains the children
    LEFT JOIN tree_coloring child1 ON parent.id = child1.parent_id AND child1.id < (SELECT MIN(id) FROM tree_coloring WHERE parent_id = parent.id)
    LEFT JOIN tree_coloring child2 ON parent.id = child2.parent_id AND child2.id > (SELECT MIN(id) FROM tree_coloring WHERE parent_id = parent.id)
    WHERE parent.id IN (
        SELECT DISTINCT parent_id 
        FROM tree_coloring 
        WHERE parent_id IS NOT NULL
    )
)
UPDATE superhero2
SET color = tc.color
FROM tree_coloring tc
WHERE superhero2.id = tc.id

INSERT INTO hero_attribute_bifurcation (hero_id, attribute_id, attribute_chunk, is_bifurcated)
SELECT 
    hero_id, 
    attribute_id, 
    attribute_chunk, 
    is_bifurcated
FROM (
    SELECT 
        hero_id, 
        attribute_id, 
        attribute_value, 
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS is_bifurcated,
        CASE 
            WHEN attribute_value > 50 THEN (attribute_value - 1) / 50 
            ELSE 0 
        END AS chunks
    FROM hero_attribute
) t
CROSS JOIN LATERAL generate_series(1, chunks) AS g
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN g = chunks THEN attribute_value - 50 * (chunks - 1) 
            ELSE 50 
        END AS attribute_chunk
) c

WITH activity_lag AS (
    SELECT 
        ts, 
        superhero_id, 
        activity_code,
        -- Calculate the difference between the current and previous timestamp
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ts - LAG(ts) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts))) / 60 as time_diff_mins
    FROM superhero_activities
),
group_flag AS (
    SELECT 
        ts, 
        superhero_id, 
        activity_code,
        -- A new group starts if there is no previous row (NULL) or if the gap is not 5 minutes
        CASE 
            WHEN time_diff_mins IS NULL OR time_diff_mins <> 5 THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END as is_new_group
    FROM activity_lag
),
group_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        ts, 
        superhero_id, 
        activity_code,
        -- Create a running total of the group flags to assign a unique ID to each consecutive block
        SUM(is_new_group) OVER (PARTITION BY superhero_id, activity_code ORDER BY ts) as group_id
    FROM group_flag
)
-- Aggregate by the group to get the start time, end time, and total duration
SELECT 
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    MIN(ts) as interval_start,
    MAX(ts) as interval_end,
    -- Sum the 5-minute intervals. Note: (count - 1) * 5 gives the duration covered by the intervals.
    -- If you want the total time span, use MAX(ts) - MIN(ts).
    (COUNT(*) - 1) * 5 as total_duration_minutes
FROM group_calc
GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, group_id
ORDER BY superhero_id, interval_start

create or replace function setup_vector_store(table_name text) returns void language plpgsql as $$
begin
    -- Drop the function if it exists to avoid "function already exists" errors
    execute 'drop function if exists match_strategies(int, jsonb, vector)';

    -- Create the table, adding the 'embedding' column required for vector operations
    execute format('create table %I ( id bigint primary key generated always as identity, description text, metadata jsonb, embedding vector )', table_name);

    -- Create the match_strategies function
    execute format('
        create function match_strategies (
            match_count int default null,
            filter jsonb default "{}"::jsonb,
            query_embedding vector
        )
        returns table ( id bigint, description text, metadata jsonb, similarity float )
        language plpgsql
        as $func$
        begin
            return query
            select
                id,
                description,
                metadata,
                1 - (embedding <=> query_embedding) as similarity
            from %I
            where metadata @> filter
            order by embedding <=> query_embedding
            limit match_count;
        end;
        $func$;', table_name);
end;
$$

WITH 
-- Generate all combinations of dimensions
all_combinations AS (
    SELECT 
        td.years,
        sd.name AS country,
        vt.name AS product_type,
        vl.name AS language,
        vg.name AS genre
    FROM time_dimension td
    CROSS JOIN spatial_dimension sd
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT id, name FROM variable WHERE name = 'ProductType') vt
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT id, name FROM variable WHERE name = 'publishing_language') vl
    CROSS JOIN (SELECT id, name FROM variable WHERE name = 'genre') vg
),
-- Map variable names to IDs for filtering
variable_ids AS (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'ProductType') AS type_id,
        (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'publishing_language') AS lang_id,
        (SELECT id FROM variable WHERE name = 'genre') AS genre_id
),
-- Aggregate variable values per datapoint
datapoint_attributes AS (
    SELECT 
        dv.datapoint_id,
        ARRAY_AGG(vv.name) FILTER (WHERE vv.variable_id = (SELECT type_id FROM variable_ids)) AS product_types,
        ARRAY_AGG(vv.name) FILTER (WHERE vv.variable_id = (SELECT lang_id FROM variable_ids)) AS languages,
        ARRAY_AGG(vv.name) FILTER (WHERE vv.variable_id = (SELECT genre_id FROM variable_ids)) AS genres
    FROM datapoint_variablevalue dv
    JOIN variablevalue vv ON dv.variablevalue_id = vv.id
    GROUP BY dv.datapoint_id
)
-- Final aggregation
SELECT 
    ac.years,
    ac.country,
    ac.product_type,
    ac.language,
    ac.genre,
    COALESCE(SUM(dp.value), 0) AS total_sales
FROM all_combinations ac
LEFT JOIN datapoints dp ON dp.time_dimension_id = (SELECT id FROM time_dimension WHERE years = ac.years) 
                        AND dp.spatial_dimension_id = (SELECT id FROM spatial_dimension WHERE name = ac.country)
LEFT JOIN datapoint_attributes da ON dp.id = da.datapoint_id
WHERE 
    (da.product_types IS NULL OR ac.product_type = ANY(da.product_types))
    AND (da.languages IS NULL OR ac.language = ANY(da.languages))
    AND (da.genres IS NULL OR ac.genre = ANY(da.genres))
GROUP BY ac.years, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre
ORDER BY ac.years DESC, ac.country, ac.product_type, ac.language, ac.genre

WITH RECURSIVE first_event AS (

tokens AS (
    SELECT 
        event_description,
        -- Generate an array of tokens from the first event description

        ARRAY(
            SELECT split_part(event_description, '#', n) 
            FROM generate_series(1, array_length(regexp_split_to_array(event_description, '#'), 1)) n
        ) AS token_array
    FROM first_event
),
prefixes AS (
    SELECT 
        -- Reconstruct the prefix string for each possible length (from longest to shortest)
        array_to_string(token_array[1:n], '#') AS prefix
    FROM tokens
    CROSS JOIN generate_series(1, array_length(token_array, 1)) AS n
    ORDER BY n DESC
)
SELECT prefix AS common_prefix
FROM prefixes p
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    -- Check if there is any event that does NOT start with the current prefix
    SELECT 1 
    FROM match_events 
    WHERE event_description NOT LIKE p.prefix || '%'
)
LIMIT 1

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION enforce_match_results_rules()
RETURNS TRIGGER AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Condition 1: Consistency
    -- If a row with the same match_id and team_id exists, the score must be the same.
    IF EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM match_results
        WHERE match_id = NEW.match_id
          AND team_id = NEW.team_id
          AND id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.id -- Exclude the current row if updating
    ) THEN
        IF NOT EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM match_results
            WHERE match_id = NEW.match_id
              AND team_id = NEW.team_id
              AND score = NEW.score
              AND id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.id
        ) THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Score mismatch: Match % and Team % already exists with a different score.', NEW.match_id, NEW.team_id;
        END IF;
    ELSE
        -- Condition 2: Uniqueness
        -- If the match_id and team_id are unique, the score must be unique across different matches.
        IF EXISTS (
            SELECT 1
            FROM match_results
            WHERE score = NEW.score
              AND id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.id
        ) THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Duplicate score: Score % already exists for another match/team.', NEW.score;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Drop the trigger if it exists to avoid conflicts
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS trigger_match_results_rules ON match_results;

-- Create the trigger
CREATE TRIGGER trigger_match_results_rules
BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE ON match_results
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE FUNCTION enforce_match_results_rules()

-- 1) Add the "status" column to the curricula table
ALTER TABLE curricula 
  ADD COLUMN status TEXT DEFAULT 'installed';

-- 2) Use a Recursive CTE to find the target curriculum and all curricula 
--    that share resources with it (transitively), then update their status.
WITH RECURSIVE curricula_to_upgrade AS (
    -- Base case: Select the specific curriculum to upgrade (e.g., ID 1)
    SELECT curriculum_id
    FROM curricula
    WHERE curriculum_id = 1 
      AND status = 'installed'

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive step: Find other curricula that share resources 
    -- with the curricula found in the previous step.
    -- Path: Current Curriculum -> Shared Resource -> Dependent Curriculum
    SELECT cr_next.curriculum_id
    FROM curriculum_resources cr_current
    JOIN curriculum_resources cr_next 
      ON cr_current.resource_key = cr_next.resource_key
    JOIN curricula_to_upgrade ctu 
      ON cr_current.curriculum_id = ctu.curriculum_id
    JOIN curricula c 
      ON cr_next.curriculum_id = c.curriculum_id
    WHERE c.status = 'installed' 
      AND cr_next.curriculum_id != ctu.curriculum_id -- Avoid cycles/self-loops
)
UPDATE curricula
SET status = 'to upgrade'
WHERE curriculum_id IN (
    SELECT curriculum_id 
    FROM curricula_to_upgrade
)

WITH RECURSIVE time_intervals AS (
    SELECT 
        id,

        resource_key,
        start_timestamp,
        end_timestamp,
        -- Generate a series of timestamps for every hour within the event duration
        generate_series(
            date_trunc('hour', start_timestamp), 
            date_trunc('hour', end_timestamp), 
            INTERVAL '1 hour'
        ) AS hour_timestamp
    FROM event_usage
    WHERE start_timestamp < end_timestamp
),
working_hours_calculation AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        hour_timestamp,
        -- Calculate the overlap between the specific hour interval and the event duration
        CASE 
            WHEN hour_timestamp + INTERVAL '1 hour' > end_timestamp THEN end_timestamp
            ELSE hour_timestamp + INTERVAL '1 hour'
        END AS interval_end,
        CASE 
            WHEN hour_timestamp < start_timestamp THEN start_timestamp
            ELSE hour_timestamp
        END AS interval_start,
        -- Check if the hour falls within Monday-Friday (0-4)
        EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM hour_timestamp) AS day_of_week
    FROM time_intervals
),
filtered_working_hours AS (
    SELECT 
        learner_code,
        resource_key,
        -- Calculate duration in seconds for the valid interval
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (interval_end - interval_start)) AS seconds_spent
    FROM working_hours_calculation
    WHERE 
        -- Filter for Monday to Friday
        day_of_week BETWEEN 1 AND 5
        AND
        -- Filter for Working Hours (09:00 to 18:00)
        -- Logic: The hour_timestamp represents the start of the hour (e.g., 09:00, 10:00).
        -- We include hours starting from 09:00 up to 17:00 (which ends at 18:00).
        EXTRACT(HOUR FROM hour_timestamp) BETWEEN 9 AND 17
)
SELECT 
    learner_code, 
    resource_key, 
    SUM(seconds_spent) AS total_time_spent
FROM filtered_working_hours
GROUP BY learner_code, resource_key

WITH RECURSIVE 
-- Calculate running totals for Buys
buy_inventory AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        date AS buy_date,
        molecules AS buy_molecules,
        usd_total AS buy_usd_total,
        -- Calculate running total of molecules bought per molecule
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date) AS buy_running_total
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 0
),
-- Calculate running totals for Sells
sell_inventory AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id,
        date AS sell_date,
        molecules AS sell_molecules,
        usd_total AS sell_usd_total,
        -- Calculate running total of molecules sold per molecule
        SUM(molecules) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id ORDER BY date) AS sell_running_total
    FROM transaction
    WHERE sell = 1
),
-- Recursive CTE to match Sells to Buys
fifo_match AS (
    -- Base Case: Match the first sell to the earliest buys
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.buy_date,
        s.sell_date,
        -- Determine how many molecules are allocated from this specific buy
        CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END AS molecules,
        -- Calculate proportional cost
        (b.buy_usd_total * (CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END) / b.buy_molecules) AS buy_usd_total,
        -- Calculate proportional revenue
        (s.sell_usd_total * (CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END) / s.sell_molecules) AS sell_usd_total,
        s.sell_running_total,
        b.buy_running_total,
        1 AS level
    FROM buy_inventory b
    JOIN sell_inventory s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    WHERE s.sell_running_total <= b.buy_running_total 
    AND (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules) < b.buy_running_total

    UNION ALL

    -- Recursive Step: Match subsequent sells to remaining buys
    SELECT 
        b.molecule_id,
        b.buy_date,
        s.sell_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END AS molecules,
        (b.buy_usd_total * (CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END) / b.buy_molecules) AS buy_usd_total,
        (s.sell_usd_total * (CASE 
            WHEN b.buy_running_total >= s.sell_running_total THEN s.sell_molecules
            ELSE b.buy_running_total - (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules)
        END) / s.sell_molecules) AS sell_usd_total,
        s.sell_running_total,
        b.buy_running_total,
        f.level + 1
    FROM buy_inventory b
    JOIN sell_inventory s ON b.molecule_id = s.molecule_id
    JOIN fifo_match f ON b.molecule_id = f.molecule_id 
        AND s.sell_running_total > f.sell_running_total
    WHERE s.sell_running_total <= b.buy_running_total 
    AND (s.sell_running_total - s.sell_molecules) < b.buy_running_total
)
-- Final Selection
SELECT 
    molecule_id, 
    buy_date, 
    sell_date, 
    molecules, 
    ROUND(buy_usd_total::numeric, 2) AS buy_usd_total, 
    ROUND(sell_usd_total::numeric, 2) AS sell_usd_total
FROM fifo_match
ORDER BY molecule_id, sell_date, buy_date

WITH attendance_counts AS (

    SELECT 
        link_to_member, 
        link_to_event, 
        COUNT(*) AS total
    FROM attendance
    GROUP BY link_to_member, link_to_event
),
ranked_events AS (
    -- Rank events for each member to find the top 2 events per member
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        total,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_member ORDER BY total DESC, link_to_event) AS rn_member
    FROM attendance_counts
),
ranked_members AS (
    -- Rank members for each event to find the top 1 member per event
    SELECT 
        link_to_member,
        link_to_event,
        total,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY link_to_event ORDER BY total DESC, link_to_member) AS rn_event
    FROM attendance_counts
)
-- Select the final results where the member is in the top 2 for that event
-- OR the event is the top 1 for that member
SELECT 
    re.link_to_member, 
    re.link_to_event, 
    re.total
FROM ranked_events re
JOIN ranked_members rm 
    ON re.link_to_member = rm.link_to_member 
    AND re.link_to_event = rm.link_to_event
WHERE re.rn_member <= 2 
   OR rm.rn_event = 1
ORDER BY re.link_to_member, re.total DESC

-- The user's request describes a scenario involving a 'student_club' database with 'members' and 'events'.
-- However, the provided database schema is for a fuel transaction system ('customers', 'gasstations', 'transactions_1k').
-- Assuming the user wants to apply the logic of "top 2 events per member" to the available schema:
-- "Top 2 events" translates to "Top 2 Gas Stations" (events where the transaction occurred).
-- "Member" translates to "Customer".
-- "Count of attendances" translates to "Count of transactions".

-- The following query finds the top 2 gas stations per customer based on the number of transactions.

WITH CustomerStationCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid, 
        gasstationid, 
        COUNT(*) as transaction_count
    FROM 
        transactions_1k
    GROUP BY 
        customerid, 
        gasstationid
),
RankedStations AS (
    SELECT 
        customerid, 
        gasstationid, 
        transaction_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customerid ORDER BY transaction_count DESC) as rank
    FROM

)
SELECT 
    customerid, 
    gasstationid, 
    transaction_count
FROM 
    RankedStations
WHERE 
    rank <= 2
ORDER BY 
    customerid, 
    transaction_count DESC

CREATE TABLE treatment_and_followup_new_backup AS
SELECT * FROM treatment_and_followup_latest;

-- Step 2: Merge the source table into the target table and delete unmatched rows
-- Note: This syntax assumes a database system that supports MERGE with DELETE (e.g., Oracle, SQL Server).
-- For PostgreSQL, this logic typically requires a separate DELETE statement or a stored procedure.

WITH RECURSIVE date_series AS (
    SELECT 
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36' AS start_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-10 15:12:24' AS end_ts,
        TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 10:34:36'::date AS current_date
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        start_ts, 
        end_ts, 
        current_date + INTERVAL '1 day'
    FROM date_series
    WHERE current_date < end_ts::date
),
business_days AS (
    SELECT 
        start_ts,
        end_ts,
        current_date,
        CASE 
            WHEN current_date = start_ts::date THEN GREATEST(0, 24 - EXTRACT(HOUR FROM start_ts) - EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM start_ts)/60 - EXTRACT(SECOND FROM start_ts)/3600)
            WHEN current_date = end_ts::date THEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM end_ts) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM end_ts)/60 + EXTRACT(SECOND FROM end_ts)/3600
            ELSE 24 
        END AS hours
    FROM date_series
    WHERE EXTRACT('ISODOW' FROM current_date) < 6
),
total_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(hours) * 3600 AS total_seconds
    FROM business_days
)
SELECT 
    FLOOR(total_seconds / 86400) || ' days ' || 
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 86400) / 3600) || ' hours ' || 
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 3600) / 60) || ' minutes ' || 
    FLOOR(MOD(total_seconds, 60)) || ' seconds ' AS time_diff
FROM total_calc

SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 
      THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour, ', MINUTE(Description), ' minutes, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) = 0 
      THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour, ', MINUTE(Description), ' minutes')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) = 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 
      THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) = 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 
      THEN CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minutes, ', SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) > 0 AND MINUTE(Description) = 0 AND SECOND(Description) = 0 
      THEN CONCAT(HOUR(Description), ' hour')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) = 0 AND MINUTE(Description) > 0 AND SECOND(Description) = 0 
      THEN CONCAT(MINUTE(Description), ' minutes')
    WHEN HOUR(Description) = 0 AND MINUTE(Description) = 0 AND SECOND(Description) > 0 
      THEN CONCAT(SECOND(Description), ' seconds')
    ELSE '0 seconds'
  END AS FormattedDuration
FROM backup_colour

WITH RECURSIVE HierarchyCTE AS (
    -- Anchor member: Select leaf nodes (nodes that are not parents of any other node)
    SELECT 
        entityid, 
        parentid, 
        emission, 
        emission AS cumulative_total
    FROM tree_table
    WHERE entityid NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT parentid FROM tree_table WHERE parentid IS NOT NULL)
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive member: Select parent nodes and calculate cumulative total
    SELECT 
        p.entityid, 
        p.parentid, 
        p.emission, 
        p.emission + (c.cumulative_total * (p.pctcontribution / 100.0)) AS cumulative_total
    FROM tree_table p
    JOIN HierarchyCTE c ON p.entityid = c.parentid
)
SELECT 
    entityid, 
    parentid, 
    cumulative_total
FROM HierarchyCTE
ORDER BY entityid

SELECT

  DisplayName,
  Age,
  SUM(DATEDIFF(LastAccessDate, CreationDate) + 1) - 
  (
    -- Count New Year's Day (Jan 1) within the user's active period
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 1) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 1) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate 
           AND MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 1) <> MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 1) THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    -- Count Independence Day (July 4) within the user's active period
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 185) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 185) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate 
           AND MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 185) <> MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 185) THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    -- Count Veterans Day (November 11) within the user's active period
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 315) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 315) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate 
           AND MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 315) <> MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 315) THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    -- Count Christmas Day (December 25) within the user's active period
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 359) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END) +
    SUM(CASE 
      WHEN MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 359) BETWEEN CreationDate AND LastAccessDate 
           AND MAKEDATE(YEAR(LastAccessDate), 359) <> MAKEDATE(YEAR(CreationDate), 359) THEN 1 
      ELSE 0 
    END)
  ) AS TotalDays

WITH RECURSIVE SetHierarchy AS (
    -- Base Case: Select all sets. 
    -- We identify the 'root' ancestor for each set.
    -- If parentCode is NULL, the root is the set itself.
    SELECT 
        code, 
        parentCode, 
        code AS rootCode
    FROM sets
    WHERE parentCode IS NULL
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive Step: Join sets with the hierarchy to find parents
    SELECT 
        s.code, 
        s.parentCode, 
        h.rootCode
    FROM sets s
    INNER JOIN SetHierarchy h ON s.parentCode = h.code
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    c.id, 
    c.name, 
    c.setCode, 
    s.parentCode, 
    h.rootCode AS ultimateAncestor
FROM cards c
INNER JOIN sets s ON c.setCode = s.code
INNER JOIN SetHierarchy h ON c.setCode = h.code

WHERE h.rootCode = 'KHM'

);

USING @NewAccounts AS Source

        -- If the difference between current date and previous date is <= 30 days, it is 'Followup'
        WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, LAG(date) OVER (PARTITION BY account_id ORDER BY date), date) <= 30 THEN 'Followup'

WITH OrderedContracts AS (
    SELECT 
        DriverId, 
        StartDate, 
        EndDate, 
        ContractType,
        -- Calculate the maximum EndDate of all previous rows for this driver and contract type
        MAX(EndDate) OVER (
            PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType 
            ORDER BY StartDate 
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ) AS PrevEndDate
    FROM driverContracts
),
GroupedContracts AS (
    SELECT 
        DriverId, 
        StartDate, 
        EndDate, 
        ContractType,
        -- Create a Group ID. 
        -- If the current StartDate is after the previous EndDate, it's a new group (increment sum).
        -- We use DATEADD(day, 1, ...) to treat consecutive dates (e.g., End=Jan 5, Start=Jan 6) as continuous.
        SUM(CASE 
            WHEN StartDate > DATEADD(day, 1, PrevEndDate) THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END) OVER (
            PARTITION BY DriverId, ContractType 
            ORDER BY StartDate
        ) AS GroupId
    FROM OrderedContracts
)
-- Aggregate the results to merge the periods
SELECT 
    DriverId, 
    ContractType,
    MIN(StartDate) AS StartDate, 
    MAX(EndDate) AS EndDate
FROM GroupedContracts
GROUP BY DriverId, ContractType, GroupId
ORDER BY DriverId, StartDate

(
    SELECT DISTINCT
        ', ' + QUOTENAME(TransType)
    FROM #TransTypes
    FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE

, 1, 2, '')

DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX) =
N'
WITH DateRange AS (
    -- Generate all dates in the range to ensure all dates appear in output
    -- even if no transactions exist for that date
    SELECT CAST(''' + CAST(@STARTDATE AS NVARCHAR) + ''' AS DATE) + number AS FullDate
    FROM master.dbo.spt_values
    WHERE type = ''P'' 
    AND CAST(''' + CAST(@STARTDATE AS NVARCHAR) + ''' AS DATE) + number <= CAST(''' + CAST(@ENDDATE AS NVARCHAR) + ''' AS DATE)
)
SELECT dr.FullDate AS Date, ' +

STUFF((
    SELECT ', COALESCE(' + QUOTENAME(TransType) + ', 0) AS ' + QUOTENAME(TransType)
    FROM #TransTypes
    FOR XML PATH(''), TYPE
).value('.', 'NVARCHAR(MAX)'), 1, 2, '') +
N'
FROM DateRange dr
LEFT JOIN 
(
    SELECT 
        [date] AS Transaction_Date,
        [type],
        COUNT(*) AS n
    FROM trans
    WHERE [date] BETWEEN CAST(''' + CAST(@STARTDATE AS NVARCHAR) + ''' AS DATE) AND CAST(''' + CAST(@ENDDATE AS NVARCHAR) + ''' AS DATE)
    GROUP BY [date], [type]
) s
PIVOT
(
    SUM(n)
    FOR [type] IN (' + @cols + ')
) pvt ON dr.FullDate = pvt.Transaction_Date
ORDER BY dr.FullDate;
'

DROP TABLE #TransTypes

EXEC sp_executesql @sql

-- The user's request involves updating a JSON string variable, not the database tables.
-- The original SQL had logic errors where it overwrote the JSON string with just the value 'F'
-- instead of modifying the JSON object, and the control flow was overly complex.
-- This corrected query simplifies the logic: check if valid JSON, check if gender is 'F', and update it.

DECLARE @info NVARCHAR(100) = '{"client_id":1,"gender":"F"}';

BEGIN TRY
    -- Check if the string is valid JSON
    IF ISJSON(@info) > 0
    BEGIN
        -- Check if the gender field exists and is 'F'
        IF JSON_VALUE(@info, '$.gender') = 'F'
        BEGIN
            -- Update the gender value to 'M'
            SET @info = JSON_MODIFY(@info, '$.gender', 'M');
        END
    END
END TRY
BEGIN CATCH
    -- Handle errors (e.g., if @info was not valid JSON)
    SET @info = '{"error": "Invalid operation"}';
END CATCH

SELECT @info AS info

WITH ZeroConsumption AS (
    -- Select only the months where consumption is 0
    SELECT 
        CustomerID, 
        Date, 
        -- Convert the string 'YYYYMM' to a Date to handle month increments correctly
        CONVERT(date, Date + '01', 112) AS FullDate
    FROM yearmonth
    WHERE Consumption = 0
),
RankedData AS (
    -- Assign a row number to each record per customer, ordered by time
    SELECT 
        CustomerID, 
        FullDate,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY FullDate) AS RowNum
    FROM ZeroConsumption
),
Islands AS (
    -- Calculate the "GroupID". 
    -- For consecutive months, the difference (in months) between the Date and the RowNum is constant.
    -- When a gap occurs, this difference changes, creating a new "Island".
    SELECT 
        CustomerID, 
        FullDate,
        DATEDIFF(month, '1900-01-01', FullDate) - RowNum AS GroupID
    FROM RankedData
)
-- Finally, group by the Customer and the GroupID to find the start and end of each continuous period
SELECT 
    CustomerID,
    MIN(FullDate) AS StartDate,
    MAX(FullDate) AS EndDate
FROM Islands
GROUP BY CustomerID, GroupID
ORDER BY CustomerID, StartDate

WITH 
-- Step 1: Merge overlapping or adjacent blackout periods into continuous blocks
merged_blackouts AS (
    SELECT 
        [start_date], 
        [end_date]
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            [start_date],
            [end_date],
            -- Calculate the maximum end_date of all periods that started before or at the same time as the current one
            MAX([end_date]) OVER (ORDER BY [start_date] ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as group_end_date
        FROM [blackout_periods]
    ) t
    -- Identify the start of a new merged block: where the current start is after the previous block's end
    WHERE [start_date] > DATEADD(DAY, -1, group_end_date)
),
-- Step 2: Generate the "gaps" between the merged blackout periods
blackout_gaps AS (
    SELECT 
        -- The gap starts the day after a blackout ends
        DATEADD(DAY, 1, [end_date]) AS [start_date],
        -- The gap ends the day before the next blackout starts
        DATEADD(DAY, -1, LEAD([start_date], 1, '9999-12-31') OVER (ORDER BY [start_date])) AS [end_date]
    FROM merged_blackouts
    WHERE [end_date] < '9999-12-31' -- Filter out the last sentinel row if necessary
    UNION ALL
    -- Add the initial gap from the beginning of time until the first blackout
    SELECT 
        '1900-01-01', 
        DATEADD(DAY, -1, MIN([start_date]))
    FROM merged_blackouts
)
-- Step 3: Find the intersection between Account Activity periods and Blackout Gaps
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN a.[start_date] < g.[start_date] THEN g.[start_date] 
        ELSE a.[start_date] 
    END AS [start_date],
    CASE 
        WHEN a.[end_date] < g.[end_date] THEN a.[end_date] 
        ELSE g.[end_date] 
    END AS [end_date]
FROM [account_activity] a
INNER JOIN blackout_gaps g
    ON a.[start_date] <= g.[end_date] 
    AND g.[start_date] <= a.[end_date]
WHERE 
    -- Ensure the resulting range is valid (start <= end)
    CASE 
        WHEN a.[start_date] < g.[start_date] THEN g.[start_date] 
        ELSE a.[start_date] 
    END <= 
    CASE 
        WHEN a.[end_date] < g.[end_date] THEN a.[end_date] 
        ELSE g.[end_date] 
    END
ORDER BY 
    [start_date], [end_date]

WITH OrderedExperiments AS (
    SELECT 
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY exp_id) as rn
    FROM 
        Experiment
    WHERE 
        mid = 2
),
InventoryCheck AS (
    SELECT 
        oe.exp_id,
        oe.mid,
        oe.qty,
        i.qty as initial_stock,
        oe.rn
    FROM 
        OrderedExperiments oe
    JOIN

),
RunningStock AS (
    SELECT 
        exp_id,
        mid,
        qty,
        initial_stock,
        initial_stock - qty as remaining_stock,
        CASE WHEN initial_stock >= qty THEN 1 ELSE 0 END as executable,
        rn
    FROM 
        InventoryCheck
    WHERE 
        rn = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    SELECT 
        oe.exp_id,
        oe.mid,
        oe.qty,
        rs.initial_stock,
        CASE 
            WHEN rs.executable = 1 THEN rs.remaining_stock - oe.qty
            ELSE rs.remaining_stock 
        END as remaining_stock,
        CASE 
            WHEN rs.executable = 1 AND rs.remaining_stock >= oe.qty THEN 1 
            ELSE 0 
        END as executable,
        oe.rn
    FROM 
        OrderedExperiments oe
    JOIN 
        RunningStock rs ON oe.rn = rs.rn + 1 AND oe.mid = rs.mid
)

WITH vt AS (
    SELECT 
        TransactionID, 
        CustomerID, 
        StartDate, 
        EndDate, 
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY CustomerID ORDER BY StartDate) as seqnum
    FROM transactions_1k
),
cte AS (
    -- Anchor member: First transaction for each customer
    -- Flag is 0, and it serves as the reference date (LastUnflaggedDate)
    SELECT 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate, 
        vt.StartDate AS LastUnflaggedDate, 
        vt.seqnum,
        0 AS flag
    FROM vt
    WHERE seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive member: Subsequent transactions
    SELECT 
        vt.TransactionID, 
        vt.CustomerID, 
        vt.StartDate, 
        vt.EndDate, 
        -- Logic: If current transaction is > 90 days from the last unflagged transaction,
        -- it becomes the new reference point (LastUnflaggedDate = StartDate).
        -- Otherwise, it inherits the previous reference point.
        CASE 
            WHEN vt.StartDate > DATEADD(day, 90, cte.LastUnflaggedDate) THEN vt.StartDate 
            ELSE cte.LastUnflaggedDate 
        END AS LastUnflaggedDate,
        vt.seqnum,
        -- Logic: If current transaction is > 90 days from the last unflagged transaction, Flag is 0.
        -- Otherwise (within 90 days), Flag is 1.
        CASE 
            WHEN vt.StartDate > DATEADD(day, 90, cte.LastUnflaggedDate) THEN 0 
            ELSE 1 
        END AS flag
    FROM cte 
    JOIN vt ON vt.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1 AND vt.CustomerID = cte.CustomerID
)
SELECT 
    TransactionID, 
    CustomerID, 
    StartDate, 
    EndDate, 
    flag
FROM cte
ORDER BY CustomerID, StartDate

        PrintCount,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY PrintCount DESC) AS rn
    FROM CardCounts
)
-- Select the top 3 cards
SELECT TOP 3
    name,
    PrintCount AS Prints
FROM RankedCards
ORDER BY PrintCount DESC

UNION ALL

-- Select the 'Others' row (sum of all cards not in the top 3)
SELECT
    'Others' AS name,
    SUM(PrintCount) AS Prints
FROM RankedCards
WHERE rn > 3

WITH hero_activity AS (

           activity_date,
           activity_value,
           hero_id
    FROM (VALUES 
        (1, '2023-01-01', 10, 1), 
        (2, '2023-01-02', 20, 1), 
        (3, '2023-02-01', 15, 1), 
        (4, '2023-02-02', 25, 1), 
        (5, '2023-01-03', 30, 2), 
        (6, '2023-02-03', 40, 2)
    ) AS t (id, activity_date, activity_value, hero_id)
),
activity_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        id,
        activity_date,
        activity_value,
        -- 1. Running total of all activity values ordered by date
        SUM(activity_value) OVER (ORDER BY activity_date) AS run_tot,
        -- 2. Running total of activity values within each month
        SUM(activity_value) OVER (
            PARTITION BY YEAR(activity_date), MONTH(activity_date) 
            ORDER BY activity_date
        ) AS m_run_tot,
        -- 3. Previous month's total activity value
        SUM(activity_value) OVER (
            ORDER BY DATEFROMPARTS(YEAR(activity_date), MONTH(activity_date), 1)
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING
        ) AS prev_m_run_tot
    FROM hero_activity
)

    activity_value,
    run_tot,
    m_run_tot,

WITH CTE_GOT AS (
    SELECT 
        ID, 
        DATE, 
        GOT,
        -- Create a grouping ID that increments whenever we encounter a non-NULL GOT value.
        -- This groups subsequent NULLs with the previous non-NULL value.
        SUM(CASE WHEN GOT IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) OVER (
            PARTITION BY ID 
            ORDER BY DATE 
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW
        ) AS Grp
    FROM Laboratory
)
SELECT 
    ID, 
    DATE, 
    GOT,
    -- Replace NULL GOT with the first (most recent) non-NULL GOT value in the group
    FIRST_VALUE(GOT) OVER (
        PARTITION BY ID, Grp 
        ORDER BY DATE
    ) AS NEW_GOT
FROM CTE_GOT

LatestLabResults AS (

PatientRiskProfile AS (
    SELECT
        PED.PATIENTID,
        PED.SEX,
        PED.BIRTHDAY,
        PED.[Examination Date],
        PED.DIAGNOSIS,
        PED.THROMBOSIS,
        CASE
            WHEN LLR.PLT < 150 OR LLR.WBC > 11 THEN 'High'

    b.county AS county2,

-- Secure and optimized approach using parameterization
-- This query safely filters for patients with 'SLE' in their diagnosis
-- without risking SQL injection by using a parameterized variable

DECLARE @TargetDiagnosis NVARCHAR(MAX) = 'SLE';

SELECT 
    p.ID,
    p.Diagnosis,
    TRIM(s.value) AS Diagnosis_Component
FROM 
    Patient p
    CROSS APPLY STRING_SPLIT(p.Diagnosis, ',') s
WHERE 
    TRIM(s.value) = @TargetDiagnosis

-- 1. Add the columns without the IDENTITY property, allowing NULLs initially
ALTER TABLE superhero ADD height_cm INT NULL, weight_kg INT NULL;

-- 2. Populate the existing rows with sequential values using ROW_NUMBER()
-- This assigns a unique sequential number to each row based on its ID
UPDATE superhero
SET height_cm = Seq.RowNum,
    weight_kg = Seq.RowNum
FROM (
    SELECT id, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS RowNum
    FROM superhero
) AS Seq
WHERE superhero.id = Seq.id;

-- 3. Alter the columns to NOT NULL (since they now have values for all existing rows)
ALTER TABLE superhero ALTER COLUMN height_cm INT NOT NULL;
ALTER TABLE superhero ALTER COLUMN weight_kg INT NOT NULL

WITH RaceResults AS (
    SELECT
        r.raceId,
        r.year,
        r.date,
        res.driverId,
        res.points,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY res.driverId, r.year ORDER BY r.date) AS SeasonRaceNumber,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY res.driverId ORDER BY r.year, r.date) AS CareerRaceNumber
    FROM results res
    JOIN races r ON res.raceId = r.raceId
    WHERE res.position IS NOT NULL
),
DriverFirstFiveRaces AS (
    SELECT
        driverId,
        points,
        CareerRaceNumber,
        CASE 
            WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 1 THEN 1
            WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 2 THEN 2
            WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 3 THEN 3
            WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 4 THEN 4
            WHEN CareerRaceNumber = 5 THEN 5
            ELSE NULL
        END AS RacePosition
    FROM RaceResults
    WHERE CareerRaceNumber <= 5
)
SELECT
    d.driverId,
    d.forename + ' ' + d.surname AS DriverName,
    MAX(CASE WHEN RacePosition = 1 THEN points END) AS Race1_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN RacePosition = 2 THEN points END) AS Race2_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN RacePosition = 3 THEN points END) AS Race3_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN RacePosition = 4 THEN points END) AS Race4_Points,
    MAX(CASE WHEN RacePosition = 5 THEN points END) AS Race5_Points
FROM DriverFirstFiveRaces r
JOIN drivers d ON r.driverId = d.driverId
GROUP BY d.driverId, d.forename, d.surname
ORDER BY d.surname, d.forename

    INNER JOIN drivers d ON res.driverId = d.driverId

WITH TeamAverages AS (
    SELECT
        AVG(CAST(buildUpPlaySpeed AS DECIMAL(10,2))) AS AvgBuildupplaySpeed,
        AVG(CAST(defenceTeamWidth AS DECIMAL(10,2))) AS AvgDefenceTeamWidth
    FROM Team_Attributes
    WHERE buildUpPlaySpeed IS NOT NULL
    AND defenceTeamWidth IS NOT NULL
),
FilteredTeams AS (
    SELECT
        TA.id,
        TA.buildUpPlaySpeed,
        TA.buildUpPlayPassing,
        TA.chanceCreationPassing,
        TA.chanceCreationCrossing,
        TA.chanceCreationShooting,
        TA.defencePressure,
        TA.defenceAggression,
        TA.defenceTeamWidth,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id ORDER BY TA.date DESC) AS LatestRecord
    FROM Team_Attributes AS TA
    CROSS JOIN TeamAverages AS AVG
    WHERE
        TA.buildUpPlaySpeed >= AVG.AvgBuildupplaySpeed AND
        TA.defenceTeamWidth <= AVG.AvgDefenceTeamWidth
)
SELECT
    id,
    buildUpPlaySpeed,
    buildUpPlayPassing,
    chanceCreationPassing,
    chanceCreationCrossing,
    chanceCreationShooting,
    defencePressure,
    defenceAggression,
    defenceTeamWidth
FROM FilteredTeams
WHERE LatestRecord = 1
ORDER BY buildUpPlaySpeed DESC, defenceTeamWidth ASC

WITH TeamStats AS (
    SELECT
        TA.ID,
        TA.team_api_id,
        TA.BUILDUPPLAYSPEED,
        TA.BUILDUPPLAYPASSING,
        TA.DEFENCETEAMWIDTH,
        TA.CHANCECREATIONSHOOTING,
        -- Use LEAD() to find the next values for each team, ordered by date
        LEAD(TA.BUILDUPPLAYSPEED) OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id ORDER BY TA.date) AS NextBuildUpPlaySpeed,
        LEAD(TA.BUILDUPPLAYPASSING) OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id ORDER BY TA.date) AS NextBuildUpPlayPassing,
        -- Use LAG() to find the previous values for each team, ordered by date
        LAG(TA.DEFENCETEAMWIDTH) OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id ORDER BY TA.date) AS PrevDefenceTeamWidth,
        LAG(TA.CHANCECREATIONSHOOTING) OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id ORDER BY TA.date) AS PrevChanceCreationShooting,
        -- Count how many records exist for each team
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY TA.team_api_id) AS TeamRecordCount
    FROM TEAM_ATTRIBUTES AS TA
)
SELECT
    ID,
    team_api_id,
    BUILDUPPLAYSPEED,
    BUILDUPPLAYPASSING,
    DEFENCETEAMWIDTH,
    CHANCECREATIONSHOOTING,
    NextBuildUpPlaySpeed,
    NextBuildUpPlayPassing,
    PrevDefenceTeamWidth,
    PrevChanceCreationShooting,
    TeamRecordCount
FROM TeamStats
-- Only include teams that have more than one record
WHERE TeamRecordCount > 1
-- And where the previous DEFENCETEAMWIDTH is greater than the current BUILDUPPLAYSPEED
  AND PrevDefenceTeamWidth > BUILDUPPLAYSPEED
ORDER BY team_api_id, ID

SELECT 
    (FLOOR(COALESCE(LEN(label), 0) / 100) * 100) AS labellengthstart,
    ((FLOOR(COALESCE(LEN(label), 0) / 100) + 1) * 100 - 1) AS labellengthend,
    COUNT(*) AS moleculecount

WITH t AS (
    SELECT 
        e.*,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.id ORDER BY e.[Examination Date]) AS seqnum
    FROM Examination AS e
),
cte AS (
    -- Anchor Member: First examination for each patient
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.[Examination Date] AS startdate,
        t.seqnum,
        'N' AS isrehire,
        t.[Examination Date] AS anchordate
    FROM t
    WHERE t.seqnum = 1
    
    UNION ALL
    
    -- Recursive Member: Subsequent examinations
    SELECT 
        t.id,
        t.[Examination Date] AS startdate,
        t.seqnum,
        CASE
            -- If more than a year has passed since the anchor, it is a rehire
            WHEN t.[Examination Date] > DATEADD(YEAR, 1, cte.anchordate) THEN 'Y'
            ELSE 'N'
        END AS isrehire,
        CASE
            -- If more than a year has passed, update the anchor to this new date
            WHEN t.[Examination Date] > DATEADD(YEAR, 1, cte.anchordate) THEN t.[Examination Date]
            ELSE cte.anchordate
        END AS anchordate
    FROM cte
    INNER JOIN t 
        ON cte.id = t.id 
        AND t.seqnum = cte.seqnum + 1
)
SELECT 
    id,
    startdate,
    seqnum,
    isrehire
FROM cte
ORDER BY id, startdate

WITH MoleculeList AS (
    SELECT value AS molecule_id
    FROM STRING_SPLIT('TR000,TR001,TR002', ',')
),
MoleculeStats AS (
    SELECT
        m.molecule_id,
        m.label,
        COUNT(a.atom_id) AS atom_count,
        COUNT(DISTINCT b.bond_type) AS unique_bond_types,
        DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(a.atom_id) DESC) AS size_rank
    FROM molecule m
    LEFT JOIN atom a ON m.molecule_id = a.molecule_id
    LEFT JOIN bond b ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
    GROUP BY m.molecule_id, m.label
)
SELECT
    ml.molecule_id,
    ms.label,
    ms.atom_count,
    ms.unique_bond_types,
    CASE
        WHEN ms.size_rank = 1 THEN 'Largest'
        WHEN ms.size_rank <= 3 THEN 'Top 3'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS size_category
FROM MoleculeList ml
LEFT JOIN MoleculeStats ms ON ml.molecule_id = ms.molecule_id
WHERE ms.atom_count > (SELECT AVG(atom_count) FROM MoleculeStats)
   OR ms.molecule_id IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT('TR000,TR001', ','))

            id,
            name,
            'ManaCost' AS label,
            manacost   AS tag1

PIVOT
(

WITH T_period_futur AS (
    SELECT 1 AS Tpf_period_id,
           CAST('2021-10-25' AS DATE) AS Tpf_start_period
    UNION ALL
    SELECT Tpf_period_id + 1 AS Tpf_period_id,
           DATEADD(WEEK, 1, Tpf_start_period)
    FROM T_period_futur
    WHERE Tpf_period_id < 12
)
SELECT Tpf_period_id,
       Tpf_start_period,
       YEAR(Tpf_start_period) AS Tpf_year,
       CAST(YEAR(Tpf_start_period) AS VARCHAR(4)) + '.' + RIGHT('00' + CAST(DATEPART(WEEK, Tpf_start_period) AS VARCHAR(2)), 2) AS Tpf_period_number,
       DATEADD(DAY, 6, Tpf_start_period) AS Tpf_end_period
FROM T_period_futur
OPTION (MAXRECURSION 0)

WITH PatientThrombosisHistory AS (
    SELECT
        e.ID,
        e.Thrombosis,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.ID ORDER BY e.[Examination Date] DESC) AS RecencyRank,
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY e.ID) AS ExamCount
    FROM Examination e
    WHERE e.Thrombosis IS NOT NULL
),
LabResults AS (
    SELECT
        l.ID,
        AVG(l.PLT) AS AvgPlateletCount,
        MAX(CASE WHEN l.PLT < 150 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS HasLowPlatelets
    FROM Laboratory l
    WHERE l.Date >= DATEADD(YEAR, -1, GETDATE())
    GROUP BY l.ID
)
SELECT
    p.ID AS PatientID,
    p.Diagnosis,
    CASE
        WHEN lr.HasLowPlatelets = 1 AND pth.Thrombosis IS NULL THEN 1
        WHEN pth.ExamCount > 1 THEN
            (SELECT TOP 1 Thrombosis
             FROM Examination
             WHERE ID = p.ID
             ORDER BY [Examination Date])
        ELSE COALESCE(pth.Thrombosis, 0)
    END AS ThrombosisStatus,
    lr.AvgPlateletCount
FROM Patient p
LEFT JOIN PatientThrombosisHistory pth ON p.ID = pth.ID AND pth.RecencyRank = 1
LEFT JOIN LabResults lr ON p.ID = lr.ID
ORDER BY p.ID

WITH TEAMATTRS AS
  (SELECT BUILDUPPLAYSPEED AS VALUE,
          'Team_Attributes' AS IDENTIFIER,
          ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                             ORDER BY BUILDUPPLAYSPEED) AS RN
   FROM Team_Attributes)
SELECT PLAYERATTRS.VALUE,
       PLAYERATTRS.IDENTIFIER,
       TEAMATTRS.VALUE,
       TEAMATTRS.IDENTIFIER
FROM
  (SELECT OVERALL_RATING AS VALUE,
          'Player_Attributes' AS IDENTIFIER,
          ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                             ORDER BY OVERALL_RATING) AS RN
   FROM Player_Attributes) AS PLAYERATTRS
INNER JOIN TEAMATTRS ON PLAYERATTRS.RN = TEAMATTRS.RN

SELECT
    DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DATEPART(WEEKDAY, CAST(originalreleasedate AS DATE)), CAST(originalreleasedate AS DATE)) AS start_of_week,
    COUNT(*) AS card_count
FROM cards
WHERE originalreleasedate IS NOT NULL
  AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, originalreleasedate) IS NOT NULL
  AND originalreleasedate >= '2007-01-01'
GROUP BY DATEADD(DAY, 1 - DATEPART(WEEKDAY, CAST(originalreleasedate AS DATE)), CAST(originalreleasedate AS DATE))
ORDER BY start_of_week

       SUM(home_team_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY DATEPART(YEAR, [date]), DATEPART(QUARTER, [date]), home_team_api_id, away_team_api_id

       SUM(home_team_goal) OVER (PARTITION BY DATEPART(YEAR, [date]), home_team_api_id, away_team_api_id
                                 ORDER BY [date] ROWS UNBOUNDED PRECEDING) AS ytd,

ORDER BY [date],

    l.format,
    MAX(CASE WHEN l.status = 'Legal' THEN l.id END) AS legal_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN l.status = 'Banned' THEN l.id END) AS banned_id,
    MAX(CASE WHEN l.status = 'Legal' THEN l.id END) - 
    MAX(CASE WHEN l.status = 'Banned' THEN l.id END) AS id_diff
FROM cards AS c
JOIN legalities AS l ON c.uuid = l.uuid
WHERE l.format IN ('commander', 'duel', 'legacy')
  AND l.status IN ('Legal', 'Banned')
GROUP BY c.name, l.format
ORDER BY c.name ASC, l.format ASC

WITH page_powers AS (
    -- Simulating the 'pages' table from the user's description
    -- In a real scenario, this would be an actual table
    SELECT 1 AS page_id, 1 AS power_id
    UNION ALL SELECT 1, 7
    UNION ALL SELECT 2, 1
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 1
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 7
    UNION ALL SELECT 3, 89
    UNION ALL SELECT 4, 20
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, 55
    UNION ALL SELECT 5, 1
    UNION ALL SELECT 6, 1
    UNION ALL SELECT 6, 13
),
page_power_counts AS (
    -- Calculate the total number of distinct powers required for each page
    SELECT 
        page_id, 
        COUNT(DISTINCT power_id) AS total_powers
    FROM page_powers
    GROUP BY page_id
)
SELECT 
    ppc.page_id,
    hp.hero_id
FROM page_power_counts ppc
JOIN page_powers pp ON ppc.page_id = pp.page_id
-- Join with the actual hero_power table from the schema
JOIN hero_power hp ON pp.power_id = hp.power_id
GROUP BY 
    ppc.page_id, 
    hp.hero_id, 
    ppc.total_powers
-- Filter to find heroes who have ALL the powers for the page
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT hp.power_id) = ppc.total_powers

WITH data AS (
    SELECT 
        p.id,
        p.[First Date] AS as_of_date,
        p.diagnosis AS current_diagnosis,
        LAG(p.diagnosis) OVER (PARTITION BY p.id ORDER BY p.[First Date]) AS prev_diagnosis,
        CASE
            WHEN p.diagnosis IN ('RA susp.', 'PSS', 'SLE') THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS output_flag
    FROM patient AS p
)
SELECT 
    as_of_date,
    id,
    current_diagnosis,
    prev_diagnosis,
    CASE
        WHEN output_flag = 1 THEN MIN(as_of_date) OVER (PARTITION BY id, output_flag ORDER BY as_of_date)
    END AS desired_output
FROM data
WHERE (current_diagnosis <> prev_diagnosis OR prev_diagnosis IS NULL)
  AND id = 2110

SELECT 
    P.ID,
    P.PLAYER_NAME AS DESCRIPTION,
    SUM(
        CASE 
            WHEN CHARINDEX('Aaron', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0 THEN 10
            WHEN CHARINDEX('Cresswell', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0 THEN 5
            WHEN CHARINDEX('Doran', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0 THEN 0.1
            ELSE 0
        END
    ) AS SCORE
FROM 
    PLAYER AS P
WHERE 
    CHARINDEX('Aaron', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0 
    OR CHARINDEX('Cresswell', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0 
    OR CHARINDEX('Doran', P.PLAYER_NAME) > 0
GROUP BY 
    P.ID, 
    P.PLAYER_NAME
ORDER BY 
    SCORE DESC

                             ORDER BY ATOM_ID) AS RN
   FROM ATOM)
SELECT MOLECULE_ID,
       MAX(CASE
               WHEN RN = 1 THEN ELEMENT
           END) AS ELEMENT1,
       MAX(CASE
               WHEN RN = 2 THEN ELEMENT
           END) AS ELEMENT2,
       MAX(CASE
               WHEN RN = 3 THEN ELEMENT
           END) AS ELEMENT3
FROM ATOMRNS
GROUP BY MOLECULE_ID

WITH DistinctBondCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id, 
        bond_type,
        COUNT(DISTINCT bond_type) OVER (PARTITION BY molecule_id) AS distinct_bond_count
    FROM bond
),
RankedBonds AS (
    SELECT 
        molecule_id, 
        bond_type,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY molecule_id 
            ORDER BY bond_type ASC
        ) AS rn
    FROM DistinctBondCounts
    WHERE distinct_bond_count > 1
)
SELECT 
    molecule_id, 
    bond_type 
FROM RankedBonds 
WHERE rn = 1 
ORDER BY molecule_id

WITH member_data AS (
    SELECT
        member_id,
        member_name,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY member_id ORDER BY member_name) AS rn
    FROM "member_trunc"
),
tokenized_members AS (
    SELECT
        a."link_to_event",
        LEVEL AS position,
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(a."link_to_member", '[^|]+', 1, LEVEL) AS member_token
    FROM "attendance_trunc" a
    CONNECT BY
        a.ROWID = PRIOR a.ROWID AND
        PRIOR SYS_GUID() IS NOT NULL AND
        LEVEL <= REGEXP_COUNT(a."link_to_member", '[^|]+')
),
member_names AS (
    SELECT
        tm."link_to_event",
        tm.position,
        COALESCE(md.member_name, 'Unknown Member') AS member_name
    FROM tokenized_members tm
    LEFT JOIN member_data md ON (tm.member_token = md.member_id AND md.rn = 1)
)
SELECT
    mn."link_to_event",
    LISTAGG(mn.member_name, '|') WITHIN GROUP (
        ORDER BY mn.position
    ) AS replaced_members
FROM member_names mn
GROUP BY mn."link_to_event"
ORDER BY mn."link_to_event"

WITH OrderedCategories AS (
    SELECT
        SUM("spent") AS category_total,
        "category"
    FROM "budget"
    GROUP BY "category"
    ORDER BY "category" DESC
),
Cumulative AS (
    SELECT category_total, category,
           SUM(category_total) OVER (ORDER BY category DESC ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cumulative_sum
    FROM OrderedCategories
)
SELECT category_total, category
FROM Cumulative
WHERE cumulative_sum <= 850
AND (cumulative_sum - category_total) < 850

FROM "event",
    (
        SELECT
            MAX(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "event_date")) AS recent_year,
            MIN(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "event_date")) AS oldest_year
        FROM "event"
    ) year_range
GROUP BY TO_CHAR("type")

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION FIND_TEST_TABLE_COLUMN(IN_COLUMN1 TEST_TABLE.COLUMN1 % TYPE, IN_COLUMN2 TEST_TABLE.COLUMN2 % TYPE, IN_COLUMN3 TEST_TABLE.COLUMN3 % TYPE, IN_COLUMN4 TEST_TABLE.COLUMN4 % TYPE, IN_REQUESTED_COLUMN VARCHAR2) RETURN VARCHAR2 IS C_TEST_TABLE SYS_REFCURSOR;RESULT VARCHAR2(255);BEGIN IF IN_COLUMN4 IS NULL THEN OPEN C_TEST_TABLE FOR SELECT * FROM TEST_TABLE WHERE COLUMN1 = IN_COLUMN1 AND COLUMN2 = IN_COLUMN2 AND COLUMN3 = IN_COLUMN3;ELSIF IN_COLUMN4 IS NOT NULL THEN OPEN C_TEST_TABLE FOR SELECT * FROM TEST_TABLE WHERE COLUMN1 = IN_COLUMN1 AND COLUMN2 = IN_COLUMN2 AND COLUMN3 = IN_COLUMN3 AND COLUMN4 = IN_COLUMN4;END IF;FOR C_TEST_TABLE_REC IN C_TEST_TABLE LOOP IF IN_REQUESTED_COLUMN = 'COLUMN1' THEN RESULT := C_TEST_TABLE_REC.COLUMN1;EXIT;ELSIF IN_REQUESTED_COLUMN = 'COLUMN2' THEN RESULT := C_TEST_TABLE_REC.COLUMN2;EXIT;ELSIF IN_REQUESTED_COLUMN = 'COLUMN3' THEN RESULT := C_TEST_TABLE_REC.COLUMN3;EXIT;END IF;END LOOP;RETURN RESULT;END

WITH all_years AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM BL.TRANSACT_DT_TM) AS year_value
    FROM BOND_LIST BL
    WHERE BL.TRANSACT_DT_TM IS NOT NULL
),
all_bond_types AS (
    SELECT BR.BOND_TYPE, BR.BOND_SEQ
    FROM BOND_REF BR
),
bond_counts AS (
    SELECT
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM BL.TRANSACT_DT_TM) AS year_value,
        BL.BOND_TYPE,
        COUNT(*) AS bond_count
    FROM BOND_LIST BL
    WHERE BL.TRANSACT_DT_TM IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM BL.TRANSACT_DT_TM), BL.BOND_TYPE
)
SELECT
    abt.BOND_TYPE,
    ay.year_value,
    NVL(bc.bond_count, 0) AS count_value
FROM all_years ay

WITH temp AS (
    SELECT 
        a."atom_id",
        a."molecule_id",
        -- Extract the numeric suffix from atom_id and convert it to a number
        TO_NUMBER(REGEXP_SUBSTR(a."atom_id", '\d+$')) AS atom_suffix,
        -- Assign a row number within each molecule_id partition, ordered by the numeric suffix
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a."molecule_id" ORDER BY TO_NUMBER(REGEXP_SUBSTR(a."atom_id", '\d+$'))) AS rn
    FROM "atom" a
),
temp2 AS (
    SELECT 
        t."atom_id",
        t."molecule_id",
        -- Calculate the difference between the row number and the numeric suffix to identify consecutive sequences
        t.rn - t.atom_suffix AS grp,
        -- Count the number of rows in each group (molecule_id and grp)
        COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY t."molecule_id", t.rn - t.atom_suffix) AS cnt
    FROM temp t
)
SELECT 
    t2."atom_id",
    t2."molecule_id"
FROM temp2 t2
WHERE t2.cnt >= 3
ORDER BY t2."molecule_id", t2."atom_id"

SELECT
    school_id,
    MIN(start_date) AS first_start,
    MAX(end_date) AS last_end
FROM
(
    SELECT
        school_id,
        start_date,
        end_date,
        SUM(gap_group) OVER (PARTITION BY school_id ORDER BY start_date) AS interval_group
    FROM
    (
        SELECT
            school_id,
            start_date,
            end_date,
            CASE
                WHEN start_date > MAX(end_date) OVER (PARTITION BY school_id ORDER BY start_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)
                THEN 1
                ELSE 0
            END AS gap_group
        FROM school_info
    )
)
GROUP BY school_id, interval_group
ORDER BY school_id, first_start
